characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: All Men Are Perverts: All three of them have displayed some interest in pornography, sexually clad women, and have tried to get laid at least once. EvenMeatwad, the most childlike and innocent of the trio, has shown hints of sexual desire. Anthropomorphic Food: They're all talking fast food products. Though in Shake's case, it's more like AnthropomorphicDrink. Anti-Hero Team: All three of them are some shade ofAnti-Hero, thoughMasterShakeis the most prevalent example by a mile. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Every so often, they'll legitimately show they care about each other and come together. It's more common between Meatwad and Frylock, but even Shake has shown he does care in his own twisted way at times. Ax-Crazy: Shake, of course, takes home the gold in terms of sheer batshit insanity, though Meatwad and Frylock definitely aren't above being a little deranged themselves. Frylock's behavior during "Fry Legs", in particular, is among the mostdisturbing that any of the three have ever exhibited.

characters.name: Master Shake
characters.description: A giant, talking milkshake, and the self-proclaimed leader of the group. He's aself-centered, profoundly insensitive, andincrediblystupidperson whothinks he's far more awesome than he actually is. Abusive Parents:To his bastard son Ezekiel, who is also a milkshake. Despite Ezekiel being a child, Shake tries to drown him as to avoid raising him, and verbally abused him for either not being good at basketball despite having never played if before, or mock him for a violation at tennis even though they're on the same team. Then it's subverted whenEzekiel isn't just not his son, but is older than him. Also, Shake is a virgin.Played Straightfor Ol' Drippy who is made of mold and other trashy materials. Despite Ol' Drippy being kind and friendly to Shake, Shake still chooses to be rude and hostile towards him and the only time he’s polite towards him is while he iseating him alive. Even afterhis deathShake shows aLack of Empathyfor his son, andblames him forgetting himself killedwhile Shake himself eats some chicken wings. To his bastard son Ezekiel, who is also a milkshake. Despite Ezekiel being a child, Shake tries to drown him as to avoid raising him, and verbally abused him for either not being good at basketball despite having never played if before, or mock him for a violation at tennis even though they're on the same team. Then it's subverted whenEzekiel isn't just not his son, but is older than him. Also, Shake is a virgin. Played Straightfor Ol' Drippy who is made of mold and other trashy materials. Despite Ol' Drippy being kind and friendly to Shake, Shake still chooses to be rude and hostile towards him and the only time he’s polite towards him is while he iseating him alive. Even afterhis deathShake shows aLack of Empathyfor his son, andblames him forgetting himself killedwhile Shake himself eats some chicken wings. Accidental Pervert: At the end of "Dickesode", Shake accidentally eats Carl's dick out of the trash, mistaking it for a hot dog.

characters.name: Frylock
characters.description: A floating, super-intelligent box of french fries with various powers. Usually the smartest and and most moral member of the trio, though he can be pretty mean when pushed too far. Adaptational Intelligence: Compared to his original "Baffler Meal" appearance where he was arguably more like Meatwad. Ambidextrous Sprite: The scar under his eye swaps sides depending on which way he's facing. Ambiguous Gender Identity: He's almost always portrayed as unambiguously male, though the first film has him grappling with the possibility that he's a lesbian, to the point where he ultimately transitions to a woman duringThe Stinger.Naturally, he's back to being male in subsequent stories. Ambiguously Brown: While he's just a red box of French fries, there are a lot of hints that he might be of African-American descent. His voice actor Carey Means is black, not to mention Shake's line of dialogue in "Boost Mobile".Shake:Yousoundblack. Where are you from?

characters.name: Meatwad
characters.description: A wad of meat. Supremely naive and just as dim-witted as Shake, but is generally portrayed as a friendly and kind-hearted lil' fella. Abandoned Catchphrase: "The bun is in your mind!" was his original catchphrase in "Baffler Meal", but this was dropped in the show proper. Achievements in Ignorance: In the finale it's revealedhe used to have a jewel like Frylock and Shake, but traded it away. Since he didn't know he needed it to live, he stayed alive. Acquired Situational Narcissism: In "Chicken and Beans", where the resulting fame of a viral video he was in causes him to betray the Aqua Teens in exchange for a rich, celebrity lifestyle, having a terrible attitude towards Frylock when asked to come home. Naturally, Meatwad's punished for this in short order. Affectionate Nickname: Carl often calls Meatwad, "Meatman" or "Little Man", as a friendly nickname.

characters.name: Chicken Bittle
characters.description: Introduced inAqua Teen Hunger Force Colon Movie Film for Theaters, Chicken Bittle is the forgotten fourth member of the Aqua Teens, who died long before the series from a lion attack. The Comically Serious: He acts like a hardened soldier and speaks in military lingo despite not being a member of the armed services, being a living chicken nugget, and surrounded by the far less serious Aqua Teens. Death by Origin Story: He was eaten by a lion in the Aqua Teens' backstory. Shake and Meatwad never noticed or even remembered him because they were too busy playing video games. Forgotten Fallen Friend: He's the long-dead fourth Aqua Teen, but Frylock rarely mentions him and Meatwad and Shake never knew he existed because they were too busy playing their Gameboys. Fusion Dance:He was created when Dr. Weird had a million chickens pressed together..

characters.name: Ol' Drippy
characters.description: A creature made of mold from the various trashy items around the house that were discarded by Master Shake. His goal is to make the Aqua Teens and everybody around them happy and content, he tries to help out as much as he can, even if it requires him to suffer. Ol' Drippy would laterlet himself get eaten by Shakein order to save the life of fellow his Aqua Teen. He made a reappearance inThe Last Onewhile trying to reform the many villains the Aqua Teens had faced. Abusive Parents: As Shake stated, he did create Ol' Drippy, but he isn’t kind to him in the slightest, and treats him only slightly better than he does Meatwad. He’s also willing toeat his sonand rejects the latte Drippy tries to gift him. Affectionate Nickname: A few characters give him the friendly nickname of Drippy. All-Loving Hero: Ol' Drippy displayed unconditional compassion to everyone, even when grouchier individuals such as Shake and Carl were rude to him, he tried to show as much kindness as possible back to them.

characters.name: Carl Brutananadilewski
characters.description: The Aqua Teens' fat, bald, middle-agedneighbor. Is regularly roped into their shenanigans, when he's not hanging out with or being harassed by them. All Men Are Perverts: He owns a crap ton of pornography and is often shown to have many bizarre sexual fetishes. He also goes to "Melon Shakers" (a strip club) very frequently, masturbates to the yoga channel, and just generally tries hard to get himself laid. Anti-Hero: An example ofUnscrupulous Hero. He's a selfish, greedy pervert even at his best, but he's generally up against people ever so slightly worse and — quite understandably — just wants to stop the various weirdos that frequent his neighborhood from stealing or breaking his property. Anti-Role Model: Not exactly on any higher moral ground than the Aqua Teens. Asshole Victim: Thanks to being amajor league prickin general, hisButt-Monkeytendencies don't always elicit sympathy.

characters.name: Dr. Weird
characters.description: A mad scientist who works out of an abandoned insane asylum in Jersey. Abusive Parents: He created the Aqua Teens... and then ordered them to fly a plane into a wall. For no apparent reason. Ass Shove: Inverted that one time.Dr. Weird:MY ASS HAS FINALLY DECIDED TOEATMY HAND!IT HUNGERS, FOR MORE!!! Ax-Crazy: He's not called Dr.Weirdfor nothing. Bad Boss: Steve is often subjected to his borderline insane and downright dangerous experiments. He's even aJerkassto Steve when he'snotdoing experiments.

characters.name: Steve
characters.description: Dr. Weird's exasperated and much-abused assistant. Beleaguered Assistant: Steve is frequently on the receiving end of Dr. Weird's antics when he'd rather not be. Butt-Monkey: He is often taken advantage of by Dr. Weird, and usually in aPlayed for Laughssort of way. Extreme Doormat: He usually goes along with Dr. Weird's abuse. Eye Scream: One episode features his eyes turning into spiders and jumping out of their sockets.

characters.name: The Mooninites (Ignignokt and Err)
characters.description: Aliens from the moon that look like they'd be better off in an Atari game. Anti-Villain: For the most part, they're usually more of an annoyance than a threat. Arch-Enemy: The most recurring enemies/nuisances of the Aqua Teens, by far. It reaches the point where they recruit the rest of the Teens'Rogues Galleryin the season 2 finale specifically to get rid of their long-time foes for good.It ends exactly as poorly as you'd expect, but y'know. Awesome, but Impractical: Their lasers can vaporize anything, but the bullets move pitifully slow. Big Guy, Little Guy: They're both short compared to most of the characters, but Ignignokt is much larger than Err.

characters.name: The Plutonians (Oglethorpe and Emory)
characters.description: Pointy aliens from Pluto. Oglethorpe is the orange one, while Emory is the green one. Achievements in Ignorance: They manage to create someone with a vague resemblance to Shake just by handing him a photo of Shake, tossing him in a pizza oven, and telling him to "wing it". Affably Evil: Emory, who is rather relaxed and passive even when executing Oglethorpe’s plans. Part of this is just because of his lack of enthusiasm for invading Earth compared to his partner. Aliens Steal Cable: Literally. They use theFargateto tap into the Aqua Teens' cable. The Bus Came Back: They make one last major appearance in the Season 10 finale. Outside of a few brief cameos here and there, the Plutonians' last major appearance was as far back as Season 4.

characters.name: MC Pee Pants
characters.description: An eccentric (and insane) rapper who keeps dying and being reincarnated in various forms; whatever form he's in, though, he always has a diaper, yellow eyes, and a shower cap, and is always scheming up aBatman Gambitto take over the world. Really, however, he just wants to be free fromSatan, the one behind his various incarnations and whom seems to be doing this to Pee Pants just for the hell of it. Ambiguously Related: Dr. Weird unveils him as his "fiancee" in theCold Openof his first episode, but it is unknown if he is a creation of Dr. Weird or just happened to be behind the door of Weird's laboratory. Notably, Dr. Weird claims to be betrayed when Steve points out that he's a giant spider. Ax-Crazy: He says he's too insane to fit into society, and he's right. He drinks people's blood, comes up with illogical, convoluted plans, andeats patio furniture. Becoming the Mask: In theAqua Donk Sidepiecesshort,MCP Pants University, he both goes through and specifically warns his viewers against this. Detailing his time spent with the old man he was trying to scam, only for him to grow emotionally attached to. Big Creepy-Crawlies: His first form was a giant spider.

characters.name: The Cybernetic Ghost of Christmas Past from the Future
characters.description: Read his name again if you're having trouble with this one.Has shown up several times to confuse the hell out of the Aqua Teens (and whoever else is listening) with "stories"/complete nonsense. Back from the Dead: Allegedly, Turkatron was The Cybernetic Ghost of Christmas Past from the Future rebuilt and sent on a mission to protect a VIP's ancestor, a laTerminator 2. It turns out he's just one of roughly 5000 malfunctioning toys that all believe themselves to be the aforementioned ghost. Bird People: He was built with a head that resemble a rooster. Catchphrase:THOOOUUSANDS OF YEARS AGO... Chewing the Scenery: Again,THOOOUUSANDS OF YEARS AGO...

characters.name: Boxy Brown, Dewey, and Vanessa
characters.description: One of Meatwad's toys who he talks to. Afro Asskicker: Subverted. He's got the afro, and he's got the attitude of a 70's Blaxploitation protagonist, but he's just a cardboard box Meatwad talks to. Alliterative Name:BoxyBrown. "Angry Black Man" Stereotype: Accuses Shake of selling out to The Man in "Shake Like Me" when Frylock paints him white, and considers reminders that he's a box (or worse, being used as one) racist.Boxy:Fry-cracker be filling me with old magazines and whatnot. I ain't no storage slave! Big, Thin, Short Trio: Boxy is the big to Dewey’s Thin and Vanessa’s short.

characters.name: Markula
characters.description: The Aqua Teens landlord. His presence makes there beContinuity. Arc Villain: He’s the main antagonist of a brief arc where the Aqua Teens are missing. Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking: The "party games" he plans to torture the Aqua Teens with include "Pin the tail on the Shake", "Bobbing for meatballs", and "Ignore the Fryman". Cranky Landlord: He's the landlord for the trio's home and considers them to be deadbeats. Daywalking Vampire: In contrast to hisWeaksauce Weakness, he seems to have no problems with sunlight.

characters.name: Rabbot
characters.description: A giant robotic rabbit created by Dr. Weird to solve “the world’s vegetable problem”, that escaped and began wreaking havoc. Animal Mecha: A large rabbit robot. Catchphrase: His most common voice clip is "Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday." He even proposes it as the name for theLegion of Doomin theVillain Team-Upepisode, and wrote it on his housewarming card for the Aqua Teens. In cursive. Invincible Villain: At no point in the series is Rabbot successfully stopped from committing wanton destruction. There have been attempts and delays, sure, but hisSuper-Toughnesspresents him has basically untouchable. Jerk with a Heart of Jerk: After the Aqua Teens move into a new house, Rabbot shows up to give them a welcoming gift. He then destroys their house.

characters.name: Mothmonsterman
characters.description: An easygoing moth-human hybrid created by Dr. Weird that Master Shake mistook for Dracula. Affably Evil: He's a pretty civil and laid-back guy. At least, when he's not laying eggs in somebody else's esophagus. Furry Reminder: Being a moth-like creature, he's attracted to light. He breaks into the Aqua Teeens house at one point because he was that desparate to get to the Shake's "Shake Signal". The Mothman: He's a half-man, half-moth creature. Moth Menace: He engulf's Carl's entire house in silk and lays eggs in Carl's esophagus all because he wanted Shake to turn his Shake Signal back on.

characters.name: Happy Time Harry
characters.description: The Alcoholic: He relies on alcohol to deal with his crippling depression. Break Him By Talking: His nihilistic speech to the cheerful Jiggle Billy about his nature as a doll was enough to make himshoot his head off.Billy survives, but is left utterly depressed. I Cannot Self-Terminate: HisAquadonk Side Piecesshort revealed that Master Shake throwing him off a cliff actually turned him into a Highlander and he's spent the past 18 years trying (and failing) to kill himself. The Cynic: Happy Time Harry has a very pessimistic view on life to say the least. Death Seeker: He gleefully embraces any chance he gets to die.

characters.name: Jimmy Tree Wizard
characters.description: A deranged homeless man who claims to be a tree wizard and has a propensity for illegal and downright insane activities. Crazy Homeless People: Pretty much his whole schtick, considering he just mumbles random nonsense in all his appearances. Non-Indicative Name: He refers to himself as a "tree wizard", but that's most likely just his insanity talking.

characters.name: Willie Nelson
characters.description: Affably Evil: He's pretty meek and polite to the main cast.He also has a taste for blood. At least he offers some to the main cast. Bad Liar: He tells a brutally mangled Shake when he asked if they scared Carl "We did it guy. High five".Shake buys it. Deadpan Snarker: He's moredeadpanthan a snarker, but he does throw out the main casts TV when Frylock tells him they threw out his mail. Giant Spider: Of a sort; he resembles an onion with spiderlike legs. I'm a Humanitarian: He rips off Carl's arms and drinks them at the end of his debut episode.

characters.name: Frat Aliens
characters.description: Two members of an alien fraternity named D.P. and Skeeter. The Alcoholic: They're almost always seen drinking and/or being drunk off their asses whenever they appear. All Men Are Perverts: They're shown to be quite perverted, openly flirting with Frylock and Meatwad (mistaking them for women) and ask where the women are during the events of "The Last One." Eaten Alive: They both get eaten by a female alien they tried to hook up with in "The Last One". Embarrassing First Name: Skeeter really doesn't like to be called Zarnold. D.P. has it slightly better with the name Donald.

characters.name: Dr. Wong Burger and Associates
characters.description: A dick with balls of rage and a coat full of mouths. Freudian Excuse: Parodied and Lampshaded in "Hands on a Hamburger".Wongburger: It took meyearsof therapy to come to terms with mytrueidentity. My parents couldn'taccept it!"My son isn't aCheeseburger!" they cried! "Your sonISACHEESEBURGER!",I CRIEDBACK!!Frylock: Look, look,I don't need to know the whole story, okay? Gag Penis: Dr. Wongburgerisa penis. And a really hammy one, at that. Large Ham: He's overly dramatic and lacks an indoor voice at times. Mad Scientist: Implied. He'sDrWongburger but none of his schemes involve actual science.

characters.name: Handbanana
characters.description: A yellow dog that Frylock genetically engineers for Meatwad using the "Make Your Own Dog 1.0" software, Carl's pool, and Master Shake's hand DNA. Abhorrent Admirer: He's got his sights set on Carl, who is both straight and not into dogs. Achilles' Heel: Just like with normal dogs, chocolate is like poison to him and he nearly ends up choking to near death on some horchata mixed with Mexican hot chocolate. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: His sinister nature can be chalked up to the side effects from the software that created him, which made it illegal in 80 countries and with a warning not to actually make your own dog. Arch-Enemy: Of all the villains featured on the show, Handbanana is the one who is Carl's most personal enemy because of his constant raping him. He has no enmity with the Aqua Teens, just Carl. It makes Carl's victory over him years after the show's original end all the sweeter.

characters.name: Jesus Ezekial Jesus
characters.description: Shake's apparent son... or is he? Continuously Crying Child: He constantly cries in his one episode due to being mistreated so much by Shake. Exit, Pursued by a Bear:Shake: You mean there aretwotalking milkshake people?Ezekial is carried away by an enraged Chess DragonFrylock: Therewere. Genius Ditz: Despite his child-like mind, he still manages to thoroughly defeat Chess Dragon at a game of chess. Hilariously Abusive Childhood: Subverted. Shake acts like a reallyAbusive Parentand doesn't want to be around him because, well, it'sShake. But then we learn that he's actually older than him.

characters.name: Carl Brain and Carl Jr.
characters.description: A duo of floating alien brains who start a car wash in order to steal other people's brains to work for them. Brain Theft: Their main goal with their business, and they're absolute idiots when it comes to thinking things through. Character Overlap: The duo originally appeared in theSpace Ghost Coast to Coastepisode "Chambraigne", the episode's title referring to a shampoo they scammed Space Ghost into buying. Did Not Think This Through: They surgically removed Carl's brain, as in the Aqua Teens neighbor, and set it down outside to try and attract more customers to the car wash, despite the fact that it can't move like they do or is even alive in the first place, resulting in Carl's brain just rotting on the side of the road next to a radio. Evil Sounds Deep: Carl Brain speaks in a low, dramatic, evil mastermind-like voice.

characters.name: The Bible Fruits
characters.description: Addled Addict: All three of them had their lives in shambles due to their drug addictions. Bert used to strangle drifters for sex,beat his fiancée, and eventually beat Tammy so much that he needed to replace his hands; Mortimerlost two fingersto his drug addiction due to rushing on a birdhouse; and Tammy was traumatized from the events that transpired during this period leading to her fearing Bert. When Bert tries to relapse, he brings down everyone with him causing the others to cooperate just to make sure he doesn’t do anymore damage. The Alcoholic: All of them are former drug addicts, but Bert has a violent relapse after he finds out Frylock has rum. After Mortimer pours it down the sink, he's actually desperate enough to drink it that he wants to find a monkey wrench and drink it out of the pipes. After that, he goes on a rampage throughout the Aqua Teens' house, demanding cocaine from his friends. Alliterative Name:BertBanana,MortimerMango, andTammyTangerine. Anthropomorphic Food: Much to the main trio's surprise, they're another trio of talking food. Frylock met them online, and assumed their pictures were just silly internet avatars. Anti-Villain: While both Tammy and Mortimer assisted in tracking down Master Shake at the end of the episode, they’re only doing so because they believe he’s filled with meth, which would allow Bert to stop abusing his fiancé, Tammy, and would generally calm him down from the destruction he’s caused.

characters.name: The Angel
characters.description: Dragged Off to Hell: His main power is sending people to hell and bringing them back, which he uses on Shake repeatedly to keep him on his leash. Good Is Not Nice: He seems to have been a pious man before being killed, but he's absolutely and understandably pissed that Shake killed him, and is bent on making his life a literal hell if he doesn't behave nice.

characters.name: Freda
characters.description: ARobot Girlbuilt by Frylock to be the perfect mate for Shake. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: Freda rather quickly realizes she can't stand Shake even though she was programmed to love him, so she dumps him and tries to convince Carl to kill him. Barbie Doll Anatomy: According to Frylock, she is not anatomically correct. As a result, her "sex tapes" show her leafing through a magazine off to the side while Shake,notenot like he has genitals eitherCarl, and Meatwad just jump up and down on the bed. Driven to Suicide: Throws herself off the bridge, after getting fed up with Shake's stalking Lady in Red: She's seen wearing a pair of red boots and shorts.

characters.name: Javier
characters.description: Dr. Weird's Hispanic janitor The Bus Came Back: He appears in the ninth season finale as the janitor of the concert. Disproportionate Retribution: In one cold opening, he picks up Dr. Weird's gold medalion, which prompts Dr. Weird to shoot him with exploding tacos.
